error_signature: Bad request - please check your parameters
title: Nextcloud connection error due to untrusted domains
symptom: When attempting to connect n8n to Nextcloud, the error 'Bad request - please check your parameters' is displayed. This prevents the retrieval of files from Nextcloud.
applies_when: Both n8n and Nextcloud are deployed in Docker
not_this_if: Nextcloud is accessible from a browser without issues
root_cause: Nextcloud requires that the requesting domains are explicitly trusted. If the domain from which n8n is trying to connect is not listed as a trusted domain in Nextcloud's configuration, it will reject the connection, resulting in a bad request error.
fix_steps: Open a terminal and execute the command to set localhost as a trusted domain: docker exec -u www-data -it php occ config:system:set trusted_domains 0 --value=localhost
Execute the command to add host.docker.internal as a trusted domain: docker exec -u www-data -it php occ config:system:set trusted_domains 1 --value=host.docker.internal
Optionally, add the container name as a trusted domain if needed.
agent_instruction: Add the necessary trusted domains in Nextcloud's configuration using the provided Docker commands.
